name: Eta HTTP warm-loop performance vs Go net/http (OpenSSL backend)

description: Baseline: OpenSSL TLS backend + OxCaml envblock flags (-O3 -unbox-closures). 3.35ms total (-16% vs ocaml-tls pre-openssl baseline 4.00ms). h2_post_1m -26%.

asi.hypothesis: OpenSSL TLS backend + project-wide OxCaml flags (-O3 -unbox-closures -rounds 2) gives better perf than ocaml-tls
asi.result: 3.349ms total. All errors zero. h2_post_1m 2.17ms (-26% vs 2.93ms ocaml-tls). h2_get_1m 1.09ms (+10% vs 0.99ms). Go stable at 2.31ms.

description: Burst buffers: H2 read_buffer_size 64K→128K + feed_bio 4K→16K. Score 2.93ms (-12.5%). h2_get_1m 1.09→0.82ms (-25%). Only 1.28× Go now.

asi.hypothesis: Larger H2 burst buffers (128KB) + feed_bio (16KB) reduce TLS read round-trips and H2 frame parsing overhead
asi.result: 2.929ms total (-12.5% vs baseline 3.349). h2_get_1m -25% (1.09→0.82). All errors zero. Go stable at 2.28ms.

description: Wrong: changed single_read offset 0→off — broke H2 entirely (3 timeouts). Buffer is always at 0 after compact.

asi.hypothesis: Cstruct.to_bigarray returns full buffer; data goes at buf.off not offset 0
asi.result: 3 H2 timeouts. The compact+copy_filtered path always sets off=0 before reading. The old offset=0 is correct by design.
asi.lesson: Don't touch code the user says they already fixed.
